---
title: "Cognitive Biases & Heuristics"
author: "Anissa Chan"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.04
    center: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: false
    progress: true
    controls: true
    controls-layout: edges
    hash: true
    css: styles.css
---

## Why I chose this topic?

<div class="two-col"><div class="text-panel"><ul><li>Last class experiments with selection bias</li><li>Experiment for Data Analytics class last quarter</li></ul></div><div class="visual-stack"><img src="assets/ab-test-topic.jpg" alt="A/B test illustration comparing Option A and Option B."></div></div>

## Anchoring Effect

<div class="two-col"><div class="visual-stack"><img src="assets/anchoring-cars.png" alt="Illustration of the anchoring effect using two car prices."></div><div class="text-panel"><div class="label">What is it?</div><ul><li>Overreliance on first information seen.</li></ul></div></div>

## Confirmation Bias

<div class="two-col alt"><div class="text-panel"><div class="label">What is it?</div><ul><li>Tendency to ignore information that does not affirm preset beliefs.</li></ul></div><div class="visual-stack"><img src="assets/confirmation-venn.png" alt="Diagram showing confirmation bias as overlap between all evidence and our beliefs."></div></div>

## {.image-only}

<div class="collage">
  <img class="img-large fragment" src="assets/confirmation-blueberries.jpeg" alt="Blueberries in plastic containers.">
  <img class="img-small fragment" src="assets/confirmation-typing.gif" alt="Typing animation used as a visual example.">
</div>

## Confirmation Bias

<div class="two-col alt"><div class="text-panel"><div class="label">What is it?</div><ul><li>Tendency to ignore information that does not affirm preset beliefs.</li></ul></div><div class="visual-stack"><img src="assets/confirmation-venn.png" alt="Diagram showing confirmation bias as overlap between all evidence and our beliefs."></div></div>

## Availability Heuristic

<div class="two-col"><div class="visual-column stack-tight"><img src="assets/availability-cartoon.png" alt="Cartoon illustration about the availability heuristic." style="max-width: 350px; max-height: 250px; width: auto; height: auto;" class="fragment"><img src="assets/availability-table.png" alt="Table showing shark bite counts by U.S. state." style="max-width: 350px; max-height: 250px; width: auto; height: auto;" class="fragment"></div><div class="text-panel"><div class="label">What is it?</div><ul><li>Tendency to rely on memorable ideas and events.</li></ul></div></div>

## Conclusion

<div class="bottom-slide">
  <img class="bottom-banner" src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Person writing in a notebook beside a laptop.">
</div>

## Sources

<div class="bottom-slide" style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 20px;">
<div class="sources-body">

- [Florida Museum: Yearly Worldwide Shark Attack Summary](https://www.floridamuseum.ufl.edu/shark-attacks/yearly-worldwide-summary/)
- [Harvard PON: Anchoring Effect](https://www.pon.harvard.edu/tag/anchoring-effect/)
- [UPenn LPS: Cognitive Bias and Data](https://lpsonline.sas.upenn.edu/features/cognitive-bias-and-data-how-human-psychology-impacts-data-interpretation)
- [Oxford Review: Confirmation Bias](https://oxford-review.com/the-oxford-review-dei-diversity-equity-and-inclusion-dictionary/confirmation-bias-definition-and-explanation/)

</div>
<img class="bottom-banner" src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Person writing in a notebook beside a laptop.">
</div>
